welcome.title: Welcome to Portfolio Manager
welcome.subtitle: A professional, multi-platform workspace for portfolio intelligence

setup.title: Configure your workspace
setup.description: Choose language, base currency, and optional API keys. Your settings sync instantly across desktop and mobile layouts.

import.title: Import from broker statements
import.description: Import CSV/PDF files from supported brokers, preview parsed rows, and handle duplicates with merge strategies before saving.

analysis.title: AI analysis and chat
analysis.description: Use Gemini-powered analysis, ask custom portfolio questions, and generate structured insights for risk, allocation, and performance.

tracking.title: Live pricing and performance
tracking.description: Track position prices with source badges and fallback logic: EODHD real-time -> FMP -> daily snapshot.

goals.title: Goals and rebalancing
goals.description: Set goal targets, follow progress, and use rebalancing tools to compare current allocation with desired weights.

market.title: Global market movers for free
market.description: Top gainers and losers are delivered daily from a public serverless snapshot. No API key is required for market movers.

security.title: Privacy-first key management
security.description: API keys are managed locally in app settings. You control providers, data refresh behavior, and when external requests are performed.

privacy.title: Your privacy, first
privacy.intro: Portfolio Manager is designed to live on your device. Before you start, take a minute to read the principles that guide how your data is handled.

local.title: Data stays on your device
local.description: Portfolios, positions, goals and settings remain local. There is no app server they get uploaded to.

keys.title: API keys protected by the OS
keys.description: Gemini, EODHD and FMP keys are encrypted by the operating-system keystore (Keychain, Android Keystore, DPAPI).

telemetry.title: No usage analytics
telemetry.description: The app sends no usage events, collects no identifiers, and ships no third-party analytics or crash-reporting SDK.

control.title: Full control over your data
control.description: You can wipe everything at any time from Settings: a single action removes portfolios, goals, cache and saved keys.

privacy.footnote: You can review this information again at any time from Settings > Review onboarding.

storage.title: Where the app stores your files
storage.intro: For full transparency, here is the exact folder layout Portfolio Manager creates on your device. Everything stays in the app's private sandbox and is removed on uninstall.

root.title: PortfolioManager/
root.description: Root folder created in the app's private documents directory. Explicit name so you can recognize what belongs to this app.

data.title: PortfolioManager/data/
data.description: Local database (Hive) with portfolios, goals and settings. Small binary files, only readable by the app.

cache.title: PortfolioManager/cache/market/
cache.description: Public market snapshots (top movers, price index) downloaded from the free pipeline. Safe to delete without losing your data.

imports.title: PortfolioManager/imports/
imports.description: Optional local copy of the broker CSV/PDF files you import, so you can re-process rows without re-selecting the file.

exports.title: PortfolioManager/exports/
exports.description: JSON/PDF backups you create. No file is written here until you explicitly request an export.

storage.footnote: API keys are NOT written to these folders: they use the OS encrypted keystore, not accessible by other apps.

network.title: Network connections
network.intro: These are the only servers Portfolio Manager talks to. Every flow is triggered by you, with no background traffic.

snapshot.title: Public market snapshot
snapshot.description: Downloads top_movers.json and prices_index.json from GitHub Pages (open-source pipeline). No key, no user profile.

gemini.title: Google Gemini (optional)
gemini.description: Only if you provide a Gemini API key: the app sends your prompts and a portfolio summary to Google for AI analysis.

eodhd.title: EODHD (optional)
eodhd.description: Only if you provide an EODHD key: real-time prices. You pay the plan and decide when to query it.

fmp.title: Financial Modeling Prep (optional)
fmp.description: Price fallback if you prefer not to use EODHD. Requires your own key and only runs on your request.

network.footnote: All network calls happen on your initiative: price refresh, AI prompt, broker import.

market.title: Market Overview
market.mover_filters_concise: Investor-grade only: price >= USD {minPrice}, dollar volume >= {dollarVolume}, no split/spike outliers. Tap for the criteria.
market.mover_filters_dialog_intro: To exclude penny stocks, pump-and-dump and changes distorted by stock splits or other corporate actions, we only show securities that pass these quality filters per timeframe:
market.mover_filters_dialog_row: Price >= USD {minPrice} - min dollar volume {dollarVolume} - max absolute change accepted: +/-{maxAbsChange}%
market.mover_filters_excluded_patterns: Also excluded: warrants, rights, units and preferred share series; for the 1M and 1Y windows a ticker must have enough trading history in the rolling database (>=22 and >=200 trading days respectively) to avoid very recent IPOs.
market.mover_filters_empty_state: No investor-grade movers on this timeframe in the current data. The filters exclude penny stocks, pump-and-dump and split-distorted moves. The list will populate at the next market data refresh.

help.title: How Rebalancing Works
help.description: This tab mirrors your portfolio positions and lets you define target weights. As you edit targets, current, target, and delta values update to show what to add or reduce.
help.step1: 1. Review each position's current allocation and value
help.step2: 2. Enter the target percentage you want for each position
help.step3: 3. Keep the total target at 100% using the summary card
help.step4: 4. Read the delta column to see how much to add or remove
help.step5: 5. "Reset to current allocation" copies current weights into all target fields
help.step6: 6. "Distribute equally" assigns the same target percentage to every position
help.step7: 7. Enable "Changes only" to show only positions where target and current differ

about.title: About
about.onboarding_reset_message: Onboarding has been reset. The app will reopen the onboarding flow.

guide.title: User Guide — Whitepaper
guide.intro: This handbook describes every area of the app in depth, from daily usage to architectural details. Designed as a reference whitepaper: scroll through the sections, tap a title to expand the technical detail.

overview.title: Overview and architecture
overview.content: What Portfolio Manager is, which platforms it runs on, and how it is built.
overview.details:
**What Portfolio Manager is**
A cross-platform application for managing your investment portfolio: import broker statements (CSV/PDF), monitor allocation and performance, set goals and rebalancing plans, read market movers and obtain AI-powered analysis on your portfolio. Everything stays on the device: no remote account, no private cloud, no telemetry.

**Supported platforms**
- Mobile: Android, iOS
- Web: Chrome, Edge, Firefox, Safari
- Desktop: Windows, Linux, macOS
The same Flutter codebase powers all platforms with responsive UI (mobile-first, automatic adaptation for desktop and tablet).

**Tech stack**
- Flutter 3 + Dart 3 for the cross-platform UI
- flutter_bloc for state management (one event → one state transition, no side-effects in widgets)
- Hive (local NoSQL) for portfolios, goals, and settings
- shared_preferences for state flags (language, onboarding completion, disclosures)
- flutter_secure_storage for API keys (Keychain on iOS, DPAPI on Windows, libsecret on Linux, Web Crypto in browsers)
- dio + retrofit + retry interceptor for HTTP
- easy_localization for the 6 supported languages (it / en / es / fr / de / pt)
- go_router for navigation, deep links and last-route restoration
- syncfusion_flutter_charts and fl_chart for visualizations

**Design philosophy**
- **Privacy-first**: no account, no telemetry, no third-party analytics. API keys and portfolio data live only on the device.
- **Open data**: free market data comes from a public, inspectable, reproducible pipeline (GitHub Actions + GitHub Pages).
- **Modular and feature-first**: each app area is isolated under `lib/features/<area>/` with its own BLoC, domain entities and presentation widgets.
- **Three-tier price robustness**: the app tries multiple sources in cascade and degrades gracefully when a key is missing or a provider is down.

**Areas map**
- Portfolio: positions, total value, allocation, treemaps
- Market: daily top movers, economic calendar
- Rebalancing: target weights and computed deltas
- Goals: targets, milestones, sync with portfolio
- AI Analysis: Gemini reports with presets and transparency panel
- Settings: language, currency, theme, API keys, data reset

getting_started.title: Getting started
getting_started.content: First-launch walkthrough: from picking a language to running your first analysis.
getting_started.details:
**First launch**
1. **Language and base currency** — at first launch you choose the interface language and the currency you will see totals and P&L in (you can change them later from Settings → General).
2. **Multi-step onboarding** — a tour of the main areas (importing, analysis, goals, market).
3. **Interactive disclosures** — three consent screens (Privacy, Local Storage, Network connections) that explain what the app does and does not do. Consent is required to proceed and versioned (`*_v1`): if policies change in the future you will review them again.
4. **Home with bottom navigation** — four tabs: Portfolio, Market, Rebalancing, Goals.

**Create your first portfolio**
- Tap "Create portfolio" to start empty and add positions manually, or
- Tap "Import" to load an export from your broker (CSV or PDF).

**Recommended setup (optional)**
- Open Settings → AI and add your Gemini API key to enable AI analysis and chat.
- Add EODHD and/or FMP keys for live quotes and price fallbacks.
The app works without any key as well: market top movers are public and free.

**Cross-device UX notes**
- On mobile: scrollable cards, adaptive spacing via flutter_screenutil, tap/long-press gestures.
- On desktop/web: same feature order, full-width layouts, hover on charts.
- Light/dark/system theme with runtime switching.

**Session restore**
If you close the app and reopen it within a few minutes, the splash takes you back to the page you were on (best-effort, falls back to Home).

importing.title: Data import and broker parsers
importing.content: How the app recognizes broker files, handles duplicates, and normalizes positions.
importing.details:
**12 brokers natively supported**
Interactive Brokers, TD Ameritrade, Fidelity, Charles Schwab, E*TRADE, Robinhood, Vanguard, DEGIRO, Trading 212, XTB, Revolut, plus a generic CSV parser as fallback.

**Parsing architecture**
- Each broker has a dedicated parser class in `lib/services/parsers/`, derived from `BaseBrokerParser`.
- `ParserFactory` acts as a registry: maps broker IDs to parsers, exposes `getParser()`, `autoParseCSV()` and `detectBroker()`.
- Auto-detect computes a score over header patterns and keywords: above threshold the broker is identified automatically.
- PDFs go through `PdfImportParser` which extracts the text, turns it into a candidate CSV, and forwards it to the parser.

**User flow**
1. Open "Import" from the Home.
2. Choose the broker profile (or leave auto-detect).
3. Upload one or more CSV/PDF files.
4. Review the position preview (symbol, quantity, price, value).
5. Pick destination: new portfolio or add to an existing one.
6. Confirm the duplicate handling strategy.

**Duplicate merge strategies**
- **Ignore**: skip rows whose symbol is already present.
- **Replace**: overwrite existing rows with the new ones.
- **Add**: aggregate quantities onto existing rows (useful for multiple statements of the same account).
Deduplication operates on normalized ISIN + symbol (case-insensitive, exchange-aware).

**Minimum required fields**
- Symbol (ticker)
- Quantity
- Price or cost basis
- Currency (recommended, otherwise inferred from locale)
- Exchange (recommended for price mapping and geographic region)

**Automatic normalization**
- EU decimals (1.234,56) and US decimals (1,234.56) auto-detected per row
- UTF-8 BOM, separators `,` `;` `\t`, multi-line headers
- Whitespace and control character trim, final deduplication

**Common errors**
- Decimal separator not recognized → check the broker export format.
- Duplicate headers or empty rows → the app filters them, but reduce them when possible.
- Missing symbol or quantity columns → manually pick the right broker profile.
- Protected or scanned PDF → use the CSV export when available (OCR is not supported).

analysis.title: AI analysis and chat with Gemini
analysis.content: How the AI engine works: presets, transparency panel, prompt builder and safeguards.
analysis.details:
**AI engine**
- Provider: Google Gemini API (`generativelanguage.googleapis.com`).
- Default model: `gemini-2.5-flash` (configurable in Settings).
- Safety: harm threshold set to HIGH for dangerous categories; controls on token count and temperature.
- Timeouts: 60s receive, 30s send, automatic retry on 5xx with exponential backoff.

**Seven analysis presets**
- **Full Review**: complete overview, holdings breakdown, diversification, risk, recommendations.
- **Risk Assessment**: focus on volatility, concentration, anomalous exposures.
- **Diversification**: balance across sectors, asset classes, regions, currencies.
- **Performance Review**: absolute and relative P&L reading, contributors and detractors.
- **Recommendations**: actionable suggestions consistent with the investor profile.
- **Geographic Exposure**: regional split and country risk.
- **Concentration Check**: hunt for dominant positions and sector Herfindahl.

**Transparency panel**
Before the request hits Gemini, the app shows exactly which portfolio "slices" make up the prompt. You can disable individual slices:
- Portfolio summary (totals, base currency)
- Holdings (position list)
- Sector allocation
- Asset class allocation
- Regional allocation
- Currency allocation
- Concentration metrics
- Performance statistics
- Performance history
- Investor profile (if filled)

**What the AI does**
- Analyzes structure, allocation and metrics of the portfolio passed as context.
- Replies in natural language in the app language.
- Can receive an additional custom prompt.

**What the AI does not do automatically**
- It does not place orders nor connect to brokers.
- It does not persist conversations on servers other than Google's (subject to provider policy).
- It does not replace a certified financial advisor.

**Multi-turn AI chat**
The chat page keeps the history for the current session with the portfolio as persistent context. You can ask for clarifications on a previous analysis, focus on a sector, or explore scenarios.

**Tips for high-quality answers**
- Refresh quotes before analyzing.
- Verify tickers (symbol mistakes confuse the AI).
- Disable slices not relevant to your question (cuts noise).
- Specific questions always beat generic ones (e.g. "what is my sector concentration risk" beats "how am I doing").

charts.title: Visualizations and charts
charts.content: Squarified treemap, geographic treemap, locale-aware tooltips, and performance charts.
charts.details:
**Positions treemap (squarified)**
Each rectangle is a position. The size is proportional to the weight on total portfolio value. The "squarified" algorithm minimizes rectangle aspect ratios for readability even on small screens. Tap or hover to see tooltips with symbol, value in base currency, allocation percentage, and P&L.

**Geographic treemap**
The same positions are grouped by exposure region: United States, Europe, Asia, Rest of World, Liquidity, Commodities, Unassigned. You can force a position's region with the `regionOverride` field in the editor (useful for global ETFs whose effective exposure differs from the domicile).

**Locale-aware tooltips**
Numbers in tooltips and legends follow the app locale: thousand/decimal separators, currency symbol, and date format are consistent with the chosen language.

**P&L color coding**
- Bright green for positions in profit
- Deep red for positions in loss
- Neutral tone for positions near breakeven
The scheme is consistent across light and dark mode with WCAG AA-compliant contrast.

**Performance charts**
For positions with available price history, the app shows a value line over time (intraday/daily depending on the provider). Markers indicate declared transactions (buys and sells, if imported).

**Reading risk from charts**
- A rectangle far larger than the others → single-position concentration risk.
- One region dominating the geographic chart → country exposure to evaluate.
- One currency weighing more than 80% → FX risk against the base currency.

goals.title: Investment goals
goals.content: Set typed goals, milestones, contributions, and track progress.
goals.details:
**Goal types**
- **Retirement** — long horizon, periodic contributions.
- **Education** — study funds (kids or yourself).
- **House** — down payment or real-estate investment.
- **Emergency** — liquid safety fund.
- **Travel** — discretionary mid-term goal.
- **Custom** — anything else you define.

**Statuses**
active · completed · paused · cancelled.

**Goal fields**
- Name and description
- Target amount and currency
- Current amount
- Target date
- Monthly contribution (optional)
- Intermediate milestones (e.g. 25% / 50% / 75%)
- Target allocation (symbol → percent mapping)

**Auto-computed metrics**
- Progress percentage
- Remaining amount
- Months to target date
- Required monthly contribution to land on time
- "On-track" flag if current contribution ≥ required contribution

**Sync with the portfolio**
The goal's target allocation can be linked to the Rebalancing module: the percent targets become the desired composition, and rebalancing deltas become an executable plan to reach the goal.

**Analytics**
The Analytics tab aggregates total goals, average completion percentages, type distribution, and timeline.

**Recommended workflow**
1. Create a "Retirement" goal with a realistic monthly contribution.
2. Define a target allocation (e.g. 60% global equities, 30% bonds, 10% cash).
3. Import or create the portfolio.
4. Open Rebalancing to see how much is missing position by position.
5. Review monthly: the "on-track" flag tells you immediately whether you're aligned.

rebalancing.title: Portfolio rebalancing
rebalancing.content: How to set target weights, compute deltas, and save an actionable plan.
rebalancing.details:
**What rebalancing is**
The process of bringing position weights back to the desired composition. The app does not place real orders: it produces a plan (deltas in value and percentage) that you can then execute manually with your broker.

**Flow**
1. Open the Rebalancing tab.
2. The app starts from the current portfolio allocation.
3. For each position, edit the **Target %** value to set the desired weight.
4. The **Delta** column shows in real time how much to add or remove.

**Calculation output**
For each position: current percent, target, delta percent, current value, target value, delta in base currency. `needsIncrease`, `needsDecrease`, `isAtTarget` flags make the next step visual.

**Quick actions**
- **Reset to current allocation** — fills targets with current weights (useful when you only want to simulate).
- **Distribute equally** — assigns the same target to all positions (e.g. 5% × 20 = 100%).
- **Changes only** — hides positions already at target so you can focus on the ones that need action.

**Validation**
The sum of targets must be 100%. A summary highlights the total delta and warns you if you're off.

**Plan persistence**
The plan is saved locally (dedicated Hive box), so you can review it after closing the app and compare with the future composition to verify execution.

**Synergy with goals**
If a goal has a target allocation, the Rebalancing module can import it with one tap: your weights stay consistent with the plan defined upstream.

market_data.title: Market data and top movers
market_data.content: Free public snapshots, investor-grade filters, price fallback chain, economic calendar.
market_data.details:
**Free movers feed (no key required)**
The app reads a daily-updated public snapshot of top gainers and top losers. The source is an open pipeline based on GitHub Actions and GitHub Pages (see Data Pipeline section).

**Available timeframes**
1 day, 1 week, 1 month, 3 months, 6 months, 1 year.

**Markets covered**
United States (NYSE, NASDAQ), UK, Germany, France, Canada, Hong Kong, Australia, India.

**Quality filters (investor-grade)**
Before ranking, every candidate must clear two gates:
- **Dollar Volume** = close price × volume ≥ 1,500,000 USD/day (eliminates illiquid names).
- **Close price** ≥ 1.00 USD (excludes penny stocks).
Positive and negative universes are sorted separately to build the gainers/losers lists.

**Position price reliability model**
When optional keys are present, the app evaluates in cascade:
1. **EODHD real-time** (if EODHD key available)
2. **EODHD end-of-day** as fallback
3. **FMP** (Financial Modeling Prep) as a further fallback
4. **Snapshot price** from the public pipeline as last resort

With no key: only the public snapshot is used, and each position shows `isPriceReliable=false` with a staleness icon.

**Safeguards**
- Staleness checks on the `as_of_date`: if the data is older than a threshold, the app flags it.
- Outlier filters: suspicious price jumps are held back.
- Ticker/exchange normalization before lookup (e.g. "VOD.L" / "VOD" / "VOD:LSE").

**Economic calendar**
If the EODHD key is configured, the Market section includes a calendar with macro events (data releases, central bank decisions, earnings), filtered by importance.

data_pipeline.title: Data pipeline (CI side)
data_pipeline.content: How market snapshots are built, distributed, and versioned.
data_pipeline.details:
**Automation stack**
Two GitHub Actions workflows and one local hook compose the "always-fresh data" loop.

**Workflow 1 — market-data-snapshot.yml**
- Trigger: cron 22:35 UTC, push on main, manual dispatch.
- Runs a Python script that uses the `EODHD_API_KEY` secret to download raw data.
- Computes top movers applying the quality filters (dollar volume ≥ 1.5M, price ≥ 1).
- Updates the rolling history database `market_history.db.zip`.
- Publishes artifacts to GitHub Pages.

**Workflow 2 — daily-data-commit.yml**
- Trigger: cron 07:00 UTC, manual dispatch.
- Downloads `top_movers.json` from Pages.
- Commits it to `main` as a daily "proof of freshness".
- Adds a row to `docs/DATA_SNAPSHOT_LOG.md` with the new snapshot date.

**Local hook — SessionStart**
- On opening a development session, the PowerShell script `.claude/hooks/session-start.ps1` runs `git pull --ff-only`.
- If local data is older than 24h, it launches `scripts/eodhd/sync_market_snapshot_from_pages.ps1` in the background (pull-only, never push) to align the local cache.

**Rolling history model**
- The Python script downloads the most recent `market_history.db.zip` from Pages at run start.
- New daily rows are upserted; missing anchors are backfilled automatically.
- Retention keeps a rolling window deep enough for 1-year comparisons.

**Published artifacts**
- `top_movers.json` — top movers + metadata on active filters (dollar volume threshold, exclusions).
- `prices_index.json` — flat ticker and `market:ticker` price lookup.
- `market_history.db.zip` — rolling history used by the pipeline (~950 MB compressed, not in git).
- Build logs as workflow artifacts.

**Operational notes**
- The app consumes published JSON and requires no local cron.
- A manual local run can reproduce the outputs for debugging.
- Heavy computation stays in CI: mobile/web execution stays lightweight.
- Source of truth: GitHub Pages. Never push the DB manually from a local session.

api_keys.title: API keys and optional providers
api_keys.content: Which keys are needed, where they are stored, what happens without them.
api_keys.details:
**The three optional keys**
- **Gemini** (Google) — AI analysis and chat. If absent, AI features are disabled but the rest of the app works.
- **EODHD** — real-time and end-of-day quotes, plus economic calendar. If absent, prices come only from the snapshot.
- **FMP** (Financial Modeling Prep) — quote fallback. Improves coverage on tickers EODHD doesn't handle well.

No key is mandatory: the app is designed to be useful even "with keys off" (public top movers, manual portfolio management).

**Where keys live**
Keys are stored in `flutter_secure_storage`, which uses the platform's native keystore:
- iOS: Keychain (encrypted with `kSecAttrAccessibleAfterFirstUnlock`).
- Android: EncryptedSharedPreferences with AES-256 key in the Keystore.
- Windows: per-user DPAPI.
- macOS: Keychain.
- Linux: libsecret / Secret Service.
- Web: Web Crypto API (with browser limits).

**Price fallback chain**
When refreshing a position's value, the app tries in order:
1. EODHD real-time → if EODHD key present and market is open.
2. EODHD end-of-day → if EODHD key present.
3. FMP → if FMP key present.
4. Public snapshot → always available, but with `isPriceReliable=false` and staleness icon.

**Connection test**
In Settings → AI / Market you can press "Test connection" to validate the entered key: the app makes a lightweight call to the provider's endpoint and gives instant feedback.

**Where to get keys**
- Gemini: Google AI Studio (`aistudio.google.com`). Generous free tier for personal use.
- EODHD: `eodhd.com` — limited free plan, paid plans for extended real-time.
- FMP: `financialmodelingprep.com` — free plan with rate limits.

**Operational best practices**
- Rotate keys periodically on the provider dashboards.
- Don't share screenshots that show the key in clear.
- On suspected compromise, revoke immediately on the provider dashboard and generate a new key.
- Data reset in Settings also clears stored keys.

privacy_security.title: Privacy and security
privacy_security.content: What the app stores, what it sends over the network, how it proves privacy compliance.
privacy_security.details:
**Interactive disclosures**
During onboarding you accept three separate disclosures, persisted in `shared_preferences` with versioned keys (`*_v1`):
- **Privacy** — local nature of the app, absence of remote accounts.
- **Storage** — which categories of data are saved on the device.
- **Network** — which network calls can be made and to which endpoints.
If policies change, version keys are bumped and you'll be asked to consent again.

**Where data lives**
Root: `<DocumentsDir>/PortfolioManager/`
- `data/` — Hive databases (settings, portfolio, goals).
- `cache/market/` — JSON market snapshots (top_movers, prices_index).
- `imports/` — copies of imported CSV/PDF files (optional, user-controlled).
- `exports/` — JSON / PDF backups exported from the app.
On Web the path is virtual and data lives in IndexedDB through Hive.

**Hive boxes**
- `settings` (theme, language, base currency, preferences)
- `portfolio` (serialized Portfolio entity)
- `goals` (serialized InvestmentGoal list)
Open-with-recovery: if a box is corrupted, it is deleted and recreated empty instead of blocking the app.

**Telemetry**
None. No third-party analytics, no remote crash reporter, no open-app beacon. The Market page makes an HTTP fetch to GitHub Pages (public origin), nothing else unless you configure API keys.

**What goes over the network (and to whom)**
- Market snapshot → public GitHub Pages endpoint.
- AI analysis → only if Gemini key is present, only to `generativelanguage.googleapis.com`, only for the portfolio slices you authorize in the transparency panel.
- Live quotes → only if EODHD/FMP key is present, only to their endpoints.
- Nothing else.

**Data reset**
From Settings → Reset data: removes all Hive boxes, shared_preferences (including disclosures), and the keys in secure storage. Reopen the app and you start from onboarding.

**User best practices**
- Keep your device encrypted (FileVault, BitLocker, default Android encryption on Android 6+).
- Don't install APKs from untrusted sources if using the mobile build.
- For the web build, avoid public shared browsers: IndexedDB persists across sessions.

metrics.title: Performance and risk metrics
metrics.content: How the app computes P&L, volatility, drawdown, Sharpe, Sortino, and concentration.
metrics.details:
**Absolute and percentage P&L**
- Absolute P&L = current value − cost basis (both converted to base currency via `fxRateToBase`).
- Percentage P&L = absolute P&L ÷ cost basis × 100.
- Computed per position and aggregated for the portfolio.
- The app preserves each position's original currency and applies the FX rate at render time.

**Allocation**
A position's percentage of total portfolio value. Computed in base currency: positions in different currencies are comparable.

**Volatility**
Standard deviation of daily returns of the position/portfolio. Requires a price time series (available for positions with provider coverage). Annualized by multiplying by √252.

**Drawdown**
Percentage decline from the local peak to the subsequent trough. The "max drawdown" is the historical maximum of these declines. An indicator of the loss tolerance required in adverse scenarios.

**Sharpe Ratio**
(Mean return − risk-free rate) ÷ total volatility. Excess return per unit of total risk. Typical numbers: <1 mediocre, 1-2 good, >2 excellent.

**Sortino Ratio**
Sharpe variant using only the deviation of negative returns in the denominator. Penalizes downside volatility while ignoring upside. More relevant for loss-averse investors.

**Beta and Alpha**
- **Beta**: sensitivity to the market benchmark. β=1 moves like the market; β>1 amplifies; β<1 dampens.
- **Alpha**: excess return over what was expected given the beta. Measures "value added" relative to passive exposure.

**Concentration**
- **Top-N weight**: aggregate weight of the top N positions (default 5).
- **Herfindahl-Hirschman Index (HHI)**: sum of the squares of percentage shares. 10,000 = monopoly (single position), 0 = perfectly diversified. Typical attention threshold: 1,500 for global equity portfolios.

**Limits**
- Time-series-based metrics need price data covered by providers (EODHD/FMP).
- Without keys, some metrics are not computed or use the public snapshot (daily granularity).
- Beta and Alpha need a coherent benchmark: the app uses a global proxy by default, configurable in the future per investor profile.

troubleshooting.title: Troubleshooting
troubleshooting.content: Quick diagnosis of the most common import, price, AI, and UI issues.
troubleshooting.details:
**Import issues**
- Check that the selected broker profile is the right one (auto-detect doesn't always nail custom CSVs).
- Remove duplicate header rows that some brokers add at the bottom of the export.
- Check the numeric format: 1.234,56 (EU) vs 1,234.56 (US). The app auto-detects, but mixed files can confuse it.
- For PDFs: use the CSV export when available; OCR of scans is not supported.
- If the preview is empty: try the "generic CSV" as a last resort.

**No live prices**
- Confirm you have saved the EODHD or FMP key in Settings.
- Press "Test connection" to verify the key is accepted.
- Check the `exchange` mapping in the position details: ambiguous tickers (e.g. "BMW" listed on multiple exchanges) need an explicit suffix.
- Manually refresh after updating the keys.

**AI analysis errors**
- Open Settings → AI and press "Test connection" to validate the Gemini key.
- Check the daily quota of the free tier on the Google AI Studio dashboard.
- Retry with a shorter prompt or disable non-essential slices in the transparency panel.
- If the error persists, check your network connection and corporate firewalls.

**Unexpected UI behavior**
- Force-restart the app (full close + reopen).
- Reset the theme from Settings → Appearance if you see inconsistent colors.
- Reopen onboarding from Settings → About if you want to review the tour.
- Clear local data only as a last resort: you'll lose unexported portfolios and goals.

**Web-specific issues**
- If IndexedDB is unavailable (incognito mode or quota exceeded), Hive can't initialize. Use a regular window and free up space.
- Aggressive ad-blockers can prevent movers loading. Whitelist `*.github.io`.

**Desktop-specific issues**
- flutter_secure_storage on Linux requires libsecret (`sudo apt install libsecret-1-dev` before build).
- On Windows, some corporate policies block DPAPI: in that case keys aren't persisted and you must re-enter them every launch.

glossary.title: Investment glossary
glossary.content: Definitions of the main terms used in the app and AI reports.
glossary.details:
**P&L** — Profit & Loss. Difference between current value and cost basis, in absolute terms or as a percentage.

**Cost Basis** — Capital originally invested in the position, pre-fees unless otherwise specified.

**Allocation** — Percentage weight of a position on total portfolio value.

**Asset Class** — Instrument category: stocks, ETFs, bonds, crypto, options, futures, commodities, cash.

**Volatility** — Measure of return dispersion over time, typically expressed as annualized standard deviation.

**Drawdown** — Percentage decline from a local peak to the subsequent trough. Max drawdown is the worst historical drop.

**Sharpe Ratio** — Excess return over risk-free, normalized by total volatility.

**Sortino Ratio** — Sharpe variant using only downside volatility in the denominator.

**Beta** — Sensitivity of the position/portfolio versus the benchmark.

**Alpha** — Excess return over what was expected given the beta — i.e. "value added" over passive exposure.

**Diversification** — Spreading risk across multiple assets, sectors, regions, and currencies to reduce concentration.

**Herfindahl (HHI)** — Concentration index: sum of squared shares. 10,000 = single position.

**Base Currency** — Currency in which the app aggregates totals and P&L. Configured in Settings → General.

**FX Rate** — Exchange rate applied to positions in a different currency to consolidate them in the base currency.

**ISIN** — International Securities Identification Number, a unique 12-character code for a financial instrument.

**Ticker** — Short instrument symbol (e.g. AAPL for Apple), exchange-specific.

**Treemap** — Hierarchical visualization in which the area of each rectangle is proportional to the size of the represented item.

**Squarified** — Treemap algorithm that minimizes rectangle aspect ratios for readability.
